title: Sun moon earth visual lexicon
wikitext:
[]: Sun/moon/earth visual lexicon             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Sat Nov 21, 2009 7:38 am http://wiki.evageeks.org/Resources:End_of_Evangelion_Screenplays Misato: "People cannot live without the mothers of life -- the Sun, Earth, and Moon. But an Angel that isn't dependent on them would be a new form of life, capable of surviving the harshest conditions." This seems like a rather key element to why the sun/moon motifs are used for Asuka and Rei, and also represented in other mindtrips..

[]: Re: Sun/moon/earth visual lexicon             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Nov 21, 2009 8:51 am FallenTabris wrote: This seems like a rather key element to why the sun/moon motifs are used for Asuka and Rei, and also represented in other mindtrips..

[]: Re: Sun/moon/earth visual lexicon             Quote Post by Allemann » Sat Nov 21, 2009 10:16 am Xard wrote: it might be as well random reference to humanity's post-HIP status

[]: Re: Sun/moon/earth visual lexicon             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sat Nov 21, 2009 12:22 pm Allemann wrote: Xard wrote: it might be as well random reference to humanity's post-HIP status

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Nov 21, 2009 1:10 pm Strictly in the visual sense... Asuka: sun (mentally bright, how outgoing she is), Rei: moon (Lilith. The Black Moon helping to create the Moon during 1I. Also the Black Moon itself), Shinji: earth (Staying grounded in Reality). Other than that, I figured the sun/moon/earth motif represents what people need to survive.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Nov 21, 2009 1:28 pm Misato is simply stating here that life on earth needs the safety of the earth, and energy from the sun. Why the moon is here explicitly is probably for poetic reasons. Essentially, Misato is stating that humans need outside natural forces in order to sustain them. Angels however, with their S^2 organs, need none of these things. Perhaps here "harshest conditions" comment was meant to suggest that Evas could be used for colonisations, or at least a different form of colonisation than, say, ordinary panspermia or colony ship exercises. In any case, we have plenty of evidence for Angels adapting to their environment. Gaghiel and Sandalphon in particular. Angles don't need a pre-existing environment capable of supporting life like humans do.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Nov 21, 2009 2:14 pm She does say, though, that an Angel who could survive outside of those things would be "a new form of life." She might've been implying that the Angels need it too.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Nov 21, 2009 2:55 pm So, along with here words that Eva was the model for this new form of life, perhaps the Angels cannot survive simply anywhere but instead permanently adapt to their environment in a potentially irreversible way? Perhaps Misato is referring to one of the earlier goals of project E. That goal might have been to create a new being that could survive indefinitely anywhere. Does this tie in to Yui's words in EoE about the Eva lasting after the sun, earth and moon are all gone? Is it this early goal that Misato is referring to, or has come across? Is this in fact Yui's final objective?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Nov 21, 2009 6:19 pm The Evangelions are clones of Adam, a Seed of Life, so you can't compare it with the normal Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Thu Aug 21, 2014 4:53 pm Sorry for the ancient topic bump but, on re-watching the series and reading through that screenplay again, the "three mothers" thing does seem kind of deliberate. Rei, Misato and Asuka are always grouped in Shinji's mind-trips (in ep. 16 as well as in EoE) as his collective view of a woman. Given the other shots where Asuka is shown with the sun and Rei with the moon, I do feel that this grouping is deliberate.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Thu Aug 21, 2014 11:36 pm What sun/moon motifs are you referring to? The only one I noticed was in the monologue in NGETV Episode 26, when Asuka laments the midday sun and Rei laments the sitting sun.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Aug 21, 2014 11:40 pm Pretty sure this ANF thread covers it: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/4438/Rei-and-the-Moon/ Can't find the thread(s) about Asuka = sun, but I know it was discussed a lot in the past.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Aug 22, 2014 10:13 am I discuss these motifs in a larger context in these threads: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/15461/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-is-an-Occult-Grimoire/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/16014/Beyond-the-Monster-Mother/ Associating Misato with the earth makes sense and I must contemplate it.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Mon Aug 25, 2014 12:46 am Compiling_Autumn wrote: What sun/moon motifs are you referring to? The only one I noticed was in the monologue in NGETV Episode 26, when Asuka laments the midday sun and Rei laments the sitting sun.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Aug 25, 2014 10:17 am In EoTV, underneath the folding chairs, they're represented by the sun/moon.

[]:             Quote Post by strawhatpatriot » Fri Aug 29, 2014 8:28 pm Asuka's plugsuit and eva are red, and hair is orange (liek the sun can be) Rei's plugsuit is white, liek the moon, and she's pretty pale.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Fri Sep 12, 2014 5:19 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: In EoTV, underneath the folding chairs, they're represented by the sun/moon.
